sections.title: Phrasal verbs
items: **apply for** — make a formal request for a job, course, etc.
**carry out** — do or complete a task
**hand in** — submit something to someone in authority
**set up** — start a business or organisation
**take on** — hire someone or accept new work

exercises.title: Topic vocabulary (gap fill)
exercises.instructions: Complete the sentences with words from the box.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete the sentences with words from the box.

items.sentence: He has to work ...... this week because the company is very busy.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is extra hours, so the correct answer is "overtime".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "overtime" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "He has to work ...... this week because the company is very busy.".

items.sentence: Read the ...... carefully before you accept the job.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is legal agreement, so the correct answer is "contract".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: After three years, she finally got a ...... and became assistant manager.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is higher position, so the correct answer is "promotion".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: My ...... is at ten o'clock tomorrow morning.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is job meeting, so the correct answer is "interview".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: You need a university ...... for this teaching job.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is degree/certificate, so the correct answer is "qualification".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: He has been ...... since the restaurant closed.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is no job, so the correct answer is "unemployed".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Past simple is wrong when the sentence links past to now (for, since, already, yet, ever).
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.

items.sentence: The hotel employs over fifty members of ...... .

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is workers, so the correct answer is "staff".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "staff" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "The hotel employs over fifty members of ...... .".

items.sentence: Her annual ...... increased when she changed jobs.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is annual pay, so the correct answer is "salary".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: Circle the correct word
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.
exercises.studentInstruction: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

items.sentenceAfter: and works as a freelance photographer.
items.originalSentence: My uncle is **self-employed / unemployed** and works as a freelance photographer.
items.completedSentence: My uncle is self-employed and works as a freelance photographer.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "self-employed" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "self-employed" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "unemployed" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.unemployed: "unemployed" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("and works as a freelance photographer.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.originalSentence: The firm plans to **apply / take on** more workers in September.
items.completedSentence: The firm plans to take on more workers in September.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "take on" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "take on" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "apply" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.apply: "apply" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("more workers in September.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.originalSentence: This is only a **permanent / temporary** job for the summer.
items.completedSentence: This is only a temporary job for the summer.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "temporary" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "temporary" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "permanent" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.permanent: "permanent" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("job for the summer.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.originalSentence: The factory pays good **staff / wages** to its workers.
items.completedSentence: The factory pays good wages to its workers.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "wages" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "wages" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "staff" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.staff: "staff" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("to its workers.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.originalSentence: She works for a small **firm / trade** near the city centre.
items.completedSentence: She works for a small firm near the city centre.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "firm" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "firm" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "trade" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.trade: "trade" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("near the city centre.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

items.originalSentence: You need previous **experience / income** to become a manager here.
items.completedSentence: You need previous experience to become a manager here.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: Only "experience" fits the meaning created by the words before and after the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "experience" collocates with the rest of the sentence. "income" would change the meaning or is the wrong register for this context.

wrongOptions.income: "income" does not match the clue in the rest of the sentence ("to become a manager here.").

explanationContent.usefulTip: When two words look similar, read the whole sentence — the clue is usually before or after the gap.

exercises.title: Phrasal verbs (complete the gaps)
exercises.instructions: Write the correct phrasal verb in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write the correct phrasal verb in each gap.

items.sentence: They want to ...... a small café near the university.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is start a business, so the correct answer is "set up".

explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

exercises.title: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each sentence with the correct form of the word in capitals.

items.sentence: The government wants to increase ...... in rural areas. (EMPLOY)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from EMPLOY, so the correct answer is "employment".

wrongOptions.employ: The root EMPLOY cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "employment".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of EMPLOY that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: EMPLOY → employment: Change the word class to a noun with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "employment" is the word-formation answer from the root EMPLOY in this context.

items.sentence: Only fully ...... nurses can apply for this position. (QUALIFY)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adjective from QUALIFY, so the correct answer is "qualified".

wrongOptions.qualify: The root QUALIFY cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "qualified".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of QUALIFY that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: QUALIFY → qualified: Change the word class to an adjective with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "qualified" is the word-formation answer from the root QUALIFY in this context.

items.sentence: Good ...... is important in every successful company. (MANAGE)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from MANAGE, so the correct answer is "management".

wrongOptions.manage: The root MANAGE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "management".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of MANAGE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: MANAGE → management: Change the word class to a noun with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "management" is the word-formation answer from the root MANAGE in this context.

items.sentence: The final ...... will be made at the end of the month. (PAY)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from PAY, so the correct answer is "payment".

wrongOptions.pay: The root PAY cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "payment".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of PAY that fits grammatically, not just the root.
explanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
explanationContent.wordFormation: PAY → payment: Change the word class to a noun with the right suffix.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "payment" is the word-formation answer from the root PAY in this context.

items.sentence: She dealt with the complaint very ...... . (PROFESSION)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adverb from PROFESSION, so the correct answer is "professionally".

wrongOptions.profession: The root PROFESSION cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "professionally".

explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of PROFESSION that fits grammatically, not just the root.

explanationContent.wordFormation: PROFESSION → professionally: Use the -ing form for an activity or gerund.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "professionally" is the word-formation answer from the root PROFESSION in this context.

items.sentence: My grandfather is enjoying his ...... after forty years of work. (RETIRE)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from RETIRE, so the correct answer is "retirement".

explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "retirement" is the word-formation answer from the root RETIRE in this context.

items.sentence: The factory needs more ...... workers to operate the machines. (SKILL)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adjective from SKILL, so the correct answer is "skilled".

wrongOptions.skill: The root SKILL cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "skilled".

explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "skilled" is the word-formation answer from the root SKILL in this context.

items.sentence: The number of ...... adults has increased in the region. (EMPLOY)

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is negative from EMPLOY, so the correct answer is "unemployed".

wrongOptions.employ: The root EMPLOY cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "unemployed".
